data.title: China Trade Surplus Beats Estimates
data.description:
China's trade surplus jumped to USD 96.18 billion in April 2025, up from USD 72.04 billion in the same period a year earlier, exceeding market expectations of USD 89 billion.

The sharp increase was largely boosted by an 8.1% year-on-year rise in exports, much faster than market forecasts of a 1.9% gain.

Meanwhile, imports unexpectedly grew by 0.8%, easily beating market expectations of a 5.9% fall, reversing a 4.3% drop in March.

data.title: Market Chatter: Putin and Xi Deepen Russia-China Strategic Ties
data.description:
Russia and China seek to deepen their strategic partnership, with presidents Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping signing a joint statement on Thursday, Reuters reported on the same day.

Putin said Russia-China relations were self-sustaining and that the two nations plan to expand the use of their national currencies in mutual settlements, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: SoundHound AI Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• SoundHound AI Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 31 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -7 cents. The mean expectation of seven analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 8 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -10 cents to -8 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 151.2% to $29.13 million from a year ago; analysts expected $30.38 million.

  • SoundHound AI Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 31 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $129.23 million.

  • SoundHound AI Inc shares had risen by 12.9% this quarter and lost 53.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 1.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the it services & consulting peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for SoundHound AI Inc is $10.00

data.title: Jameson Resources Says Review of Canada Coal Project Bankable Feasibility Study Endorses Validity; Shares Rise 33%
data.description:
Jameson Resources said that independent technical advisers reviewed its bankable feasibility study for the Crown Mountain hard coking coal project in British Columbia, Canada, and the competent person for the study remains valid based on the review of the updated information, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Since the release of the study in 2020, there have been inflationary increases in both capital and operating costs in Canada as well as in competitor producer locations, the filing said.

The review identified the range of capital and operating costs increases based on the repricing of key capital equipment items and the adoption of published increases to labor, fuel, construction, and infrastructure costs.

The outcomes from the review using the updated economic inputs resulted in an estimated increase of 28% to the project's pre-production capital from $309 million to $394 million, as well as a 15% increase in cash operating costs from $89.41 per tonne in the yield optimization study to $102.79 per tonne.

The review also considered the impact of revised coal price forecasts and exchange rates.

The firm's shares jumped over 33% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Enel Reports Higher Q1 Attributable Profit, Total Revenue
data.description:
Enel (ENEL.MI) said Thursday its profit and revenue increased year over year in the first quarter.

Profit attributable to owners of the parent for the three months ended March 31 was 2.01 billion euros, compared with 1.93 billion euros earlier. EPS was unchanged at 0.19 euro.

The Italian energy company's total revenue was 22.07 billion euros, compared with 19.43 billion euros a year ago.

For full-year 2025, the group expects net ordinary income between 6.7 billion euros and 6.9 billion euros. For 2027, net ordinary income is expected to be in the range of 7.1 billion euros to 7.5 billion euros.

data.title: Locksley Resources Files Drilling Application for US Project; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Locksley Resources submitted the drill permit application for the Desert Antimony Mine, within its Mojave project in California, to the US Bureau of Land Management, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Sampling at the mine yielded "exceptional" results, including 46% antimony from rock chip assays, with eight samples above 17% antimony, and mineralization mapped across a 400-meter surface strike length, the filing said.

The company plans to conduct further exploration in high-grade antimony and rare earth element zones through surface and subsurface methods, per the filing.

Shares were up 5% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: Australia's Orica extends gain; brokerages see benefits from pricing, demand growth
data.description:
** Shares of Orica rise as much as 2.6%, extending gains to A$18.48, their highest level since December 10, 2024

** Mining and infrastructure solutions provider posts strong H1 earnings, declares higher interim dividend and restarts buy-back

** UBS sees sustained EPS growth driven by global mine demand and ammonium nitrate (AN) prices

** Morgan Stanley says co well positioned to capitalise on attractive industry structures, strong demand in mining end-markets

** We view ORI to benefit from pricing growth in domestic AN markets and further penetration of value-added products - MS

** Stock set for 9.8% weekly gain, fourth consecutive weekly rise

News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Stocks Gain After Trump Touts U.K. Deal, High Hopes on China

The Dow industrials climb 0.6%; Nasdaq adds 1.1%.

Trump's Bespoke Trade Deal With U.K. Sets Little Precedent for Other Nations

Many other agreements weren't seen as likely to come together quickly.

Bitcoin rallies with stocks to top $100,000 again as exuberance returns to markets

Bitcoin rose above $100,000 for the first time since February, reigniting hopes that the crypto may climb to a new record high.

With U.K. Deal, U.S. Signals That 10% Tariff on World Is New Baseline

Both countries are hailing this as a good outcome, showing how much the landscape has shifted.

RBA to Walk Rate-Cut Path With Dangers on Both Sides

The Reserve Bank of Australia is set to cut rates and keep on cutting until the official cash rate is at a far less restrictive level.

Trump's Tariffs Turned a Sleepy Industry Into the Front Lines of the Trade War

For years, customs brokers have helped companies calculate tariffs and expedite imports. The trade war has turned their business into a game of whack-a-mole.

New York Fed Survey Finds Consumers Ambivalent on Inflation Outlook

Consumers sent mixed signals about their inflation expectations in the New York Fed's April survey. But the poll showed no serious rise in long-term inflation expectations.

Canada's Financial Stability Faces Stiff Test From U.S. Tariffs, Bank of Canada Says

Annual central bank report says there is a risk permanent tariffs could cause high unemployment and business insolvencies.

Why the Fed Isn't Ready to Join Other Central Banks in Cutting Rates

The U.S. faces risks of higher inflation and unemployment. Countries without tax hikes on imports are focused on the hit to growth.

EU Targets American Aircraft, Car Parts for Possible Tariffs if Talks Fail

The bloc said it could target more than $100 billion worth of American products with tariffs if negotiations with the U.S. break down.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Lyft Swings to Profit Amid Increase in Ridership

San Francisco-based company said rides grew 16% year over year, to 218.4 million, ahead of analyst views for 215.1 million.

McKesson to Spin Off Medical-Surgical Company

The pharmaceutical distributor said the new company would be a differentiated medical surgical supply and solutions business.

CoStar Agrees to Buy Australian Advertiser Domain for $1.9 Billion

U.S. real-estate analytics provider CoStar agreed to buy property advertiser Domain in a sweetened deal.

News Corp Revenue Boosted by Gains at Dow Jones, HarperCollins

Digital and print Wall Street Journal subscriptions totaled 4.34 million for the March quarter, the company says.

Paramount Notches Streaming Growth, but Revenue Drops

The company saw gains in its streaming business, but its overall revenue declined compared with the same period last year when it televised the Super Bowl.

Peloton Slashes Marketing Again in Hard Play for Profitability

Double-digit percentage cuts continue for the fourth consecutive quarter as the fitness company's marketing chief leaves.

Coinbase Reports Drop in Profit

Earlier in the day Coinbase announced a $2.9 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire Deribit, a bitcoin options exchange.

TKO Group Raises Full-Year Outlook on Recent Acquisitions, Swings to Profit

The new forecasts came as TKO Group swung to a profit and posted higher-than-expected revenue in the recent quarter.

Air Canada Loss Widens, Full-Year Outlook Lowered on Economic Uncertainty

Chief Executive Michael Rousseau said he expects market conditions to be unsteady moving forward as the economic outlook appears uncertain

Funko Pulls Outlook as Loss Widens, Sales Fall

The company said it accelerated plans to diversify its supply chain, especially with regard to imports from China.

data.title: Fraser & Neave's Attributable Profit Rises Marginally in Fiscal H1
data.description:
Fraser & Neave's attributable profit to shareholders rose 0.3% to SG$84.1 million in the six months ended March 31, from SG$83.2 million a year earlier, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

Earnings per share after exceptional items declined slightly to SG$0.057 from SG$0.058 in the comparable period.

Revenue was up 13% year over year to SG$1.21 billion from SG$10.7 billion, due to strong sales form the beverages segment.

data.title: Umm Al Qaiwain General Investments' Q1 Net Profit, Total Revenue Rise
data.description:
Umm Al Qaiwain General Investments (ADX:QIC) said Thursday that its net profit and total revenue for the first quarter rose year over year.

Net profit after tax for the three months ended March 31 was 12.2 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 11 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.034 dirham from 0.030 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed investment company's total revenue was 13 million dirhams, compared with 12.7 million dirhams earlier.

data.title: China Apr Exports +8.1% on Year; Expected +2.5%
Japanese Yen Weakens as Dollar Rebounds
data.description:
The Japanese yen weakened past 145 per dollar, hovering at a one-month low as the US dollar gained ground amid improving global trade sentiment and fading expectations of imminent US rate cuts.

The greenback’s strength was buoyed by US President Donald Trump’s announcement of a preliminary trade deal with the UK, the first since the implementation of broad US tariffs last month.

Trump also hinted at more trade agreements and suggested that tariffs on China could be eased, depending on the outcome of high-level trade talks scheduled for this weekend in Switzerland.

Further pressuring the yen, US Fed Chair Powell dismissed the idea of a preemptive rate cut, pointing to persistent inflationary risks and concerns about the labor market.

On the domestic front, data showed that personal spending in Japan rose more than expected in March, offering a positive signal for consumption.

However, a third consecutive monthly decline in real wages cast a shadow over the broader economic outlook.

Market Chatter: China Exempts Some US Imports from Retaliatory Tariffs
data.description:
China has excluded certain pharmaceuticals, microchips, and aircraft engines from its 125% tariffs on US goods, with sources saying it has created a "whitelist" that allowed some imports to be free from retaliatory tariffs, Reuters reported Thursday.

Among the goods excluded were diagnostic reagents and products, certain analog chips, some aircraft parts including jet engines, and ethane imports, according to the report.

data.title: China says it has full confidence in ability to manage US trade issues
data.description:
China has full confidence in its ability to manage U.S. trade issues, Vice Foreign Minister Hua Chunying said on Friday, a day before officials from both sides are set to meet in Switzerland to discuss the tariffs they have imposed on each other.

"We have no fear," Hua told a small group of reporters at a middle school in a rural county in southwestern China, adding that the trade policy of the U.S. administration cannot be sustained.

The weekend talks involving top U.S. and Chinese economic and trade officials are widely seen as a first step towards resolving a trade war that has disrupted the global economy. President Donald Trump said on Thursday that the U.S. tariffs on Beijing of 145% would likely come down.

"We have full confidence," Hua said during a Beijing-organised trip to Malipo county to showcase China's efforts to build up rural economies.

"We do not want any kind of war with any country. But we have to face up to the reality. As you can see, people have full confidence in our capability to overcome all the difficulties."

Trump's tariffs on many of the United States' trading partners, including China, are increasingly weighing on a world economy which for decades had benefited from predictable and relatively free trade.

Many economists are calling the Trump tariffs a "demand shock" to the world economy which, by making imports more expensive for American businesses and consumers, will sap activity elsewhere.

"What the United States is doing cannot be sustained," Hua said. "Ordinary people in the U.S. already feel suffering from the tariff war."

The U.S. administration will come back to "normal", she said.

data.title: China Apr Trade Surplus CNY690B
LG Electronics' India Unit Begins Construction of New Manufacturing Facility in Andhra Pradesh
data.description:
The Indian unit of LG Electronics began the construction of its new manufacturing facility in Andhra Pradesh's Sri City, the South Korean electronics products maker said in a Thursday release.

LG Electronics India's $600 million investment will create about 1,495 direct jobs. The facility, LG's third plant in India, spread over 247 acres and will produce air conditioner compressors, refrigerators, washing machines, and air conditioners, the release said.

Operations are expected to begin by the end of 2026.

data.title: China Imports Unexpectedly Rise
data.description: China’s imports grew by 0.8% yoy in April 2024, reversing a 4.3% drop in the prior month and marking the first increase since December.

data.title: China Apr Imports +0.8% on Year in Yuan Terms
China Apr Exports +9.3% on Year in Yuan Terms
Japan's Nikkei hits more than one-month high on optimism around trade talks
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average hit a more than one-month high on Friday, as risk appetite was lifted by hopes of progress in U.S. trade talks and domestic firms' better-than-expected outlook.

The Nikkei rose 1.49% to 37,478.58 by the midday break, its highest level since March 27, and is set to post a 1.76% weekly gain.

The broader Topix rose 1.46% to 2,738, and was set for an 11-session rally - its longest since October 2017.

"Investors see that the market slump in April was the worst, and the environment not just for equities but for bonds is only getting better as more compromises on trade talks could be possible," said Hiroyuki Ueno, chief strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Asset Management.

U.S. President Donald Trump and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Thursday announced a limited bilateral trade agreement that leaves in place Trump's 10% tariffs on British exports.

Financial markets are now awaiting the outcome of preliminary U.S.-China trade talks due to begin on Saturday in Switzerland.

Trump said on Thursday he expects there to be substantive negotiations between the two countries, and predicted that punitive U.S. tariffs on Beijing of 145% would likely come down.

"The market was also relieved that the outlook of Japanese firms, including Toyota, is not severely affected by the U.S. tariffs," said Ueno.

Investors were once pessimistic about the corporate outlook amid uncertainties about the impact of U.S. tariffs.

Among individual stocks, NTT Data surged 14% after NTT said it would take the subsidiary private by purchasing the shares it does not already own at 4,000 yen per share.

Ajinomoto rose 6.24% after the food and healthcare company announced a 100-billion-yen ($686.2 million) share buyback.

On the other hand, air-conditioning maker Daikin Industries fell 5.03% to become the worst percentage loser on the Nikkei, and also weighed on the Nikkei the most.

($1 = 145.7300 yen)

data.title: Daiwa House Logistic Trust's NPI Up 2.7% in Q1
data.description:
Daiwa House Logistic Trust's net property income or NPI rose 2/7% in Q1 to SG$11.1 million from SG$10.8 million a year earlier, according to a Friday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Distributable income declined nearly 10% year over year to SG$8.2 million from SG$9.2 million.

Total portfolio occupancy stood at 92.1%.

data.title: Iron ore rangebound as investors weigh Sino-US trade talks, slow demand
data.description:
Iron ore futures traded in a tight range on Friday as investors weighed prospects of easing Sino-U.S. trade tensions against seasonally weak demand from top consumer China.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) traded 0.43% lower at 697 yuan ($96.14) a metric ton, as of 0241 GMT.

While the benchmark June iron ore (SZZFM5) on the Singapore Exchange was up 0.45% at $96.95 a ton.

U.S. President Donald Trump predicted that punitive U.S. tariffs on Beijing of 145% would likely come down, the latest sign of a softening tone between the two superpowers.

The United States have unveiled details of a new trade agreement with Britain.

But analysts and traders maintained a cautious stance ahead of the Sino-U.S. talks this weekend.

While near-term ore demand remained resilient, signs of weakening downstream steel consumption have threatened to hinder any upside room, said analysts.

Average daily hot metal output - typically used to gauge iron ore demand - nudged 0.1% higher to 2.46 million tons week-on-week, as of May 8, the highest since October 2023, a survey from consultancy Mysteel showed.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE lost further ground, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) down 1.23% and 1.15%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange retreated. Rebar shed 0.98%, hot-rolled coil lost 0.75%, wire rod (SWRcv1) fell 1.95% and stainless steel edged 0.16% lower.

($1 = 7.2496 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Indian Rupee Weakens Amid Escalating Tensions With Pakistan — Market Talk
data.description: The Indian rupee weakens against U.S. dollar in the Asian session amid escalating geopolitical tensions between India and Pakistan. India said it intercepted missiles and drones launched by Pakistan on Thursday that targeted military installments on its territory, turning up the heat in a confrontation between the two nuclear-armed countries. "There could be a case for RBI to lean against excessive depreciation pressures, given the relatively sharp move in INR," says Chang Wei Liang, FX & credit strategist at DBS Group Research, in commentary. USD/INR is 0.4% higher at 85.8400, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)
